{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[Kumamoto Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyushu Shinkansen]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[Kumamoto Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyushu Shinkansen]].

The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[Kumamoto Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyushu Shinkansen]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[Kumamoto Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyushu Shinkansen]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[Kumamoto Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyushu Shinkansen]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[Kumamoto Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyushu Shinkansen]].

Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[:en:Kumamoto Station|Kumamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyushu Shinkansen|Kyushu Shinkansen]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[:en:Kumamoto Station|Kumamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyushu Shinkansen|Kyushu Shinkansen]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[:en:Kumamoto Station|Kumamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyushu Shinkansen|Kyushu Shinkansen]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/76 Kumamoto-to-Heart home page]}{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]]. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from [[:en:Kumamoto Station|Kumamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kyushu Shinkansen|Kyushu Shinkansen]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kumamoto-guide.jp/spots/detail/76 Kumamoto-to-Heart home page]}{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Segonkō Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Segonkō Kofun | native_name =千金甲古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Segonkō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto|Kumamoto, Kumamoto]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|47|04|N|130|37|51|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite}} stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}}. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from {{ill|Kumamoto Station|en|Kumamoto Station|WD=Q969324|lt=Kumamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|WD=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from {{ill|Kumamoto Station|en|Kumamoto Station|WD=Q969324|lt=Kumamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|WD=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen}}.

{{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun'''|千金甲古墳 |}} are two [[Kofun period]] [[decorated kofun]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Kojima Shitamachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kumamoto, Kumamoto| Kumamoto]], {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were separately designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]s in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139654|title=千金甲古墳（乙号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206683|title=千金甲古墳（甲号）|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (A)'''|千金甲古墳 (乙号) |Segonkō kofun (otsugō)}} is located at the western edge of the Kumamoto Plain, south of the Gongenyama Hills that extend south from Mount Kinpu, at the saddle between Mt. Takagi and the south of the Gongenyama Hills, at an elevation of about 60 meters. The mouths of the Tsuboi River and Shirakawa River can be seen below. It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from {{ill|Kumamoto Station|en|Kumamoto Station|WD=Q969324|lt=Kumamoto Station|1=Kumamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|WD=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}}.

It is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular end circular tumulus with a 2.6 meter horizontal entry passageway. The stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is 3.6 by 3 meters wide, and although the height is unclear as the ceiling was opened early, it is estimated to have been over 4 meters. The burial chamber is made of broken {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|WD=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} stones stacked flat, and at the back there is a huge stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}}. The sarcophagus is 1.17 meters high, with an inner width of 1.75 meters and depth of 1 meters. The inside is painted with triple concentric circles as well as bows, quivers, boats, swords, and other designs, and is painted red and green. It is estimated to have been built in the mid-6th century. The tumulus was covered by a roof in 1960 (Showa 35) to protect the interior.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The {{nihongo|'''Segonkō Kofun (B)'''|千金甲古墳 (甲号) |Segonkō kofun (kōgō)}} is located at a higher elevation of 110 meters on the southern slope of the Gongenyama Hills on the western edge of Kumamoto City. The surrounding area is home to several other ''kofun'' clusters, including the Terayashiki ''kofun'' cluster, Gongendaira ''kofun'' cluster, Narazakiyama ''kofun'' cluster, and Takajoyama ''kofun'' cluster, and this is the highest of the five ''kofun'' in the Segonkō ''kofun'' cluster. The mound was carved into a semi-circular ditch along the slightly gentle slope of the mountain (northwest slope), and the soil was then piled up. It is a circular tomb orientated to the southwest. Although the entrance passage is buried, it is about 3 meters long. The stone burial chamber is made of broken andesite stones laid flat, measuring 2.5 by 2.7 meters. The interior of the burial chamber is surrounded by a stone barrier made of six [[tuff ]]slabs, the inner walls of which are engraved with concentric circles, diagonal lines and quiver patterns, and are painted in three colors: red, blue and yellow. The tomb as robbed in ancient times, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} remain, but it is estimated to have been built around the beginning of the 6th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumuli are about 6.8 kilometers east from {{ill|Kumamoto Station|en|Kumamoto Station|WD=Q969324|lt=Kumamoto Station|1=Kumamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|WD=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}}.
